workItems.title: Manifest validation and resolution contract
workItems.goal: Validate manifest-defined apps and resolve optional Playbook defaults through one deterministic CLI boundary.
acceptanceCriteria: Manifest validation runs through the Lifeline CLI boundary.
Playbook defaults remain optional and file-based.
Explicit manifest values override Playbook defaults.

workItems.title: Single-machine runtime lifecycle
workItems.goal: Operate one manifest-defined app on one machine with deterministic lifecycle and restore behavior.
acceptanceCriteria: Runtime commands cover up, down, status, logs, restart, and restore.
Supervisor state and logs remain local and explicit.
Port ownership and crash-loop failures fail closed.

workItems.title: Bounded release lifecycle
workItems.goal: Plan, persist, activate, and roll back immutable single-host releases with explicit pointer safety.
acceptanceCriteria: Release planning is non-mutating.
Persisted releases are immutable and replayable.
Activation and rollback preserve confirmation and lineage evidence.

workItems.title: Deterministic runtime and release receipts
workItems.goal: Emit auditable receipts for governed execution, proof, release, and rollback outcomes.
acceptanceCriteria: Receipts identify action, status, provenance, and remediation.
Release replay can detect malformed, duplicate, or inconsistent evidence.
Owner verification covers receipt contracts.

workItems.title: Current multi-OS startup backend set
workItems.goal: Register the canonical lifeline restore entrypoint on the currently supported operating systems.
acceptanceCriteria: Windows, Linux, macOS, FreeBSD, OpenBSD, NetBSD, and AIX have explicit backends.
Unsupported platforms report contract-only status.
Dry-run remains non-mutating.

workItems.title: Capability-backed privileged execution bridge
workItems.goal: Execute bounded read-only and dry-run worker requests with explicit capability and approval receipts.
acceptanceCriteria: Capability mismatch and expired or rejected approval fail closed.
Out-of-scope workspace writes do not execute.
Every accepted attempt emits an auditable receipt.

workItems.title: ATLAS UI proof receipt bridge
workItems.goal: Convert clean, completion-ready ATLAS UI proof into deterministic Lifeline proof receipts.
acceptanceCriteria: Blocked or mismatched proof cannot emit a passed receipt.
Path-like source references normalize across operating systems.
Receipt failure identifies a first remediation step.

workItems.title: Owner roadmap and platform-cutover reconciliation
workItems.goal: Replace the empty verification plan as roadmap evidence and reconcile historical cutover claims against shipped owner truth.
acceptanceCriteria: A keyed machine roadmap separates completed capabilities from non-complete work.
The empty .playbook plan is classified as verification output rather than product roadmap authority.
Non-complete work exports deterministically without Discord mutation.

workItems.title: External runtime-home placement contract
workItems.goal: Place all mutable Lifeline state beneath an explicit runtime home without redirecting application or repository path semantics.
acceptanceCriteria: Global --root and LIFELINE_ROOT inputs resolve by CLI, environment, then invoking-cwd precedence.
Runtime, log, startup, release, privileged-execution, and proof-receipt state remains beneath the resolved home's .lifeline directory.
Detached supervisors inherit the resolved absolute LIFELINE_ROOT value.
Doctor reports the resolved home and state directory without creating them.
Application working directories, Atlas discovery, and unrelated repository paths remain based on their existing contracts.

workItems.title: Evaluate a startup backend for a measured unsupported platform
workItems.goal: Add another startup installer only after a real target platform and operational need are identified.
acceptanceCriteria: A concrete unsupported target platform is named from current operator evidence.
The backend uses the canonical lifeline restore entrypoint.
Status, dry-run, install, uninstall, and deterministic tests follow the existing startup contract.
No generic all-platform expansion is authorized.

workItems.title: Supervised Playbook Observer restart proof
workItems.goal: Prove that the Playbook Observer can be started and restored under Lifeline supervision using the canonical external runtime home.
acceptanceCriteria: Playbook start:lifeline wiring supplies the canonical Lifeline runtime-home argument.
Observer runtime paths remain separate from Lifeline supervisor state.
A bounded supervised restart proves state, log, pid, and health continuity.
The source checkout remains free of mutable .lifeline state.

workItems.title: Windows logon restore contract and proof
workItems.goal: Prove Windows logon restoration for the supervised Playbook Observer after the supervised restart contract is established.
acceptanceCriteria: The Windows startup backend restores through the canonical lifeline restore entrypoint.
The restored process inherits the canonical external Lifeline runtime home.
Logon proof records startup intent, task state, supervisor state, and Observer health.
Registration and proof remain bounded to the named Windows operator lane.
